text.title: AI Coding Agent Handoff Protocol Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding AI Coding Agent Handoff Protocol. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every agent run is preceded by a TASK.md hand-off — verbal-only briefs are rejected.
rule.rationale: Every agent run is preceded by a TASK.md hand-off — verbal-only briefs are rejected.

rule.statement: TASK.md names allowed file paths and forbidden changes; agent must not write outside.
rule.rationale: TASK.md names allowed file paths and forbidden changes; agent must not write outside.

rule.statement: TASK.md contains ≥1 binary AC the reviewer can check.
rule.rationale: TASK.md contains ≥1 binary AC the reviewer can check.

rule.statement: Diff cannot merge without recorded human review verdict.
rule.rationale: Diff cannot merge without recorded human review verdict.

rule.statement: TASK.md names how to roll back if review fails.
rule.rationale: TASK.md names how to roll back if review fails.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-ai-coding-agent-handoff-protocol.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
